scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE PRINCIPAL CITY CIVIL JUDGE, BENGALURU

ORIGINAL SUIT NO. _____ OF 2024

Ram Kumar,
S/o {{FATHER_NAME_PLAINTIFF}},
Aged about 45 years,
R/o Jayanagar, Bengaluru, Karnataka – {{PINCODE_PLAINTIFF}}.     ... PLAINTIFF

VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
S/o {{FATHER_NAME_DEFENDANT}},
Aged about 38 years,
R/o Koramangala, Bengaluru, Karnataka – {{PINCODE_DEFENDANT}}.     ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY

1. The plaintiff is a resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, and is a person of good standing and reputation.

2. The defendant is a resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, and is known to the plaintiff.

3. On 15.03.2024, the plaintiff advanced a hand loan of Rs. 15,00,000 (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the defendant. The said amount was transferred via NEFT from the plaintiff's bank account to the defendant's bank account, bearing UTR No. AXIB20240315123456, dated 15.03.2024.

4. There was no written agreement between the parties regarding the said loan. The loan was advanced on the basis of oral agreement and mutual understanding between the parties, at the request of the defendant.

5. The defendant acknowledged the loan through WhatsApp messages sent to the plaintiff, thereby confirming the existence and receipt of the loan amount.

6. The plaintiff made repeated oral demands upon the defendant to repay the loan amount. However, the defendant has failed and neglected to repay the same.

7. The plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to the defendant, calling upon him to repay the loan amount within a reasonable time. The defendant received the said legal notice but has failed to comply with the same.

8. The defendant has committed default in repayment of the loan amount with effect from 20.06.2024.

9. The plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the principal amount from the date of default, i.e., 20.06.2024, until realization.

STATEMENT OF ACCOUNT

10. The statement of account showing the amount due and payable by the defendant to the plaintiff is as under:

(i) Principal loan amount advanced on 15.03.2024: Rs. 15,00,000
(ii) Part-payments made by defendant: Nil
(iii) Balance principal amount due: Rs. 15,00,000
(iv) Interest @ 12% p.a. from 20.06.2024 till filing: To be computed
(v) Total amount due and payable: Rs. 15,00,000 + Interest

VALUATION AND JURISDICTION

11. The suit is valued for the purpose of court fee and jurisdiction at Rs. 15,00,000 (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) plus interest.

12. The court fee payable on this plaint is as per the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, and the plaintiff pays the requisite court fee of Rs. ______________.

13. This Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction to try and entertain this suit as the defendant resides within the territorial limits of Bengaluru, and the cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court.

14. Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 provides that the Civil Court shall have jurisdiction to try all suits of a civil nature excepting suits of which the cognizance is either expressly or impliedly barred. The present suit being one for recovery of money is a civil suit triable by this Hon'ble Court.

15. Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 provides that every suit shall be instituted in a Court within the local limits of whose jurisdiction the defendant resides or carries on business or works for gain, or where the cause of action, wholly or in part, arises. The defendant resides at Koramangala, Bengaluru, and the cause of action arose within the territorial limits of this Hon'ble Court.

CAUSE OF ACTION

16. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 15.03.2024 when the plaintiff advanced the loan amount to the defendant via NEFT transfer and the defendant acknowledged the receipt of the same.

17. The cause of action further arose on 20.06.2024 when the defendant committed default in repayment of the loan amount, despite oral demands made by the plaintiff.

18. The cause of action further arose on 01.07.2024 when the plaintiff issued a legal notice to the defendant calling upon him to repay the loan amount, and the defendant failed to comply with the same despite receipt of notice.

LIMITATION

19. The present suit is within limitation as prescribed under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from the date when the loan is made. The loan was advanced on 15.03.2024 and the present suit is being filed well within the prescribed period of three years.

INTEREST

20. The plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the principal amount from the date of default, i.e., 20.06.2024, until the date of filing of the suit, as pre-suit interest.

21. Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 empowers the Court to award interest at such rate as the Court deems reasonable on the principal sum adjudged in the suit from the date of the suit to the date of the decree (pendente lite
